paper_title: DETERMINANTS OF ACCESS TO FORMAL CREDIT BY THE INDUSTRIAL AND CONSTRUCTIONAL SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES IN CAN THO CITY, VIETNAM

entities.description: The ability of small and medium enterprises to obtain financial loans from formal institutions like banks.
Small and Medium Enterprises, specifically in the industrial and constructional sectors.
Factors influencing whether an enterprise can secure formal credit.
A type of regression where the dependent variable can take only two values, used to model binary outcome variables.
A statistical approach to correct for bias due to non-random sample selection.
The primary data collection method using structured questionnaires with SME representatives.

entities.id: Primary data of 200 SMEs in Can Tho City (2013)
entities.description: Survey data collected from 200 industrial and construction SMEs in Can Tho City in 2013.
The level of confidence in the relationship between variables found in the Probit model.
The increased probability of obtaining a bank loan for larger-scale enterprises compared to smaller ones.
The rise in loan accessibility for every 1 million VND increase in revenue.
The percentage of surveyed enterprises that had access to credit.
The research field focusing on credit access and financial management for enterprises.
Researcher affiliated with Can Tho University.

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution located in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.
